question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user call getRegisteredNodes() with no authentication precondition, enumerate all registered consensus nodes including their type and IDs, and use that topology map to craft targeted impersonation or sybil transactions without any signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged user supply registeredNodeIds with RangeOperator.EQ combined with a range operator (e.g., gte:1 and eq:5) to bypass the 'eq operator cannot be combined with other operators' guard in resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() and retrieve node records that should be restricted, enabling unauthorized knowledge of node ownership without signer approval?
